headerDisplay: Mammotion cloud control using PyMammotion. Use a dedicated Mammotion account shared to your mower(s).

email.title: Email
email.description: Mammotion account email (or account number if your account requires it).

pythonPath.title: Python path
pythonPath.description: Optional. Leave EMPTY to use the managed Python 3.13 venv. Only set this to override with your own interpreter.

areaNameFallbacks.title: Area name fallbacks
areaNameFallbacks.description: Optional fallback area names when Mammotion does not return map/plan areas. Use mower device name as key (or '*' for default), e.g. {"Luba-VAFFT58A":["Front Lawn","Back Lawn"]}.

cloudRefreshSeconds.title: Cloud refresh cadence (seconds)
cloudRefreshSeconds.description: How often the plugin refreshes the mower state from the Mammotion cloud (drives how fast an externally-started mow is detected). Lower = faster, but more cloud traffic. Firmware below 1.30.25.1 is rate-limited — keep this at ~120s or higher; on 1.30.25.1+ you can go as low as 30s.

enableMatterRvc.title: Enable Matter Robot Vacuum
enableMatterRvc.description: Use Matter Robotic Vacuum Cleaner accessory when Matter is available and enabled in Homebridge; otherwise fallback to HomeKit switch.

exposeBattery.title: Show battery on the Matter vacuum
exposeBattery.description: Reports battery % + charging state on the Matter Robotic Vacuum tile (Power Source cluster). No extra tile.

deviceFilter.title: Device filter
deviceFilter.description: Optional list of exact device names to expose, e.g. Luba-123456.

enableStateSensors.title: Expose HomeKit state sensors (for automations)
enableStateSensors.description: Adds Docked / Mowing / Problem contact sensors you can trigger automations on (Apple Home cannot trigger on the Matter vacuum's state directly).

sensorDocked.title: • Undocked sensor (docked = closed)
sensorDocked.description: Contact sensor named 'Undocked'. CLOSED while docked (Angedockt = geschlossen — no warning ~90% of the time), OPEN when away/mowing. Trigger on 'closes' = re-docked.

sensorBladeWear.title: • Blade Service sensor (blade wear)
sensorBladeWear.description: Contact sensor that OPENS when blade usage reaches the mower's warning threshold. Off by default.

sensorProgress.title: • Mow progress sensor (%)
sensorProgress.description: Mow completion % as a humidity sensor (Apple Home shows it as 'Humidity' — rename the tile). Off by default.

errorIncludesSensorFaults.title: Treat bumper/ultrasonic faults as a problem
errorIncludesSensorFaults.description: Also open the Problem sensor when a bumper or ultrasonic sensor reports ERROR (e.g. stuck on an obstacle).

sensorDebounceSeconds.title: Sensor debounce (seconds)
sensorDebounceSeconds.description: Minimum time a state must hold before a sensor flips. 0 disables. Problems are reported immediately.

enableAbortSwitch.title: Expose momentary 'Abort Mowing' switch
enableAbortSwitch.description: ENDS the current job and returns the mower to its dock. Auto-resets. Off by default (destructive).

enablePlanSwitches.title: Expose a 'Run <plan>' switch per saved plan
enablePlanSwitches.description: Adds one momentary switch per saved mowing plan (e.g. 'Run Daily'). Tapping it starts that specific plan; also works as an automation trigger. Off by default.

defaultPlan.title: Default plan for the Play button
defaultPlan.description: When several saved plans exist, the plan the RVC Play button runs. Leave empty to use the first plan (alphabetical). Match the plan/task name from the Mammotion app, e.g. 'Daily'.

enableEveHistory.title: Eve history on the contact sensors
enableEveHistory.description: Logs open/close history (Undocked / Mowing / Problem / Blade) for graphing in the Eve app. Apple Home ignores it. On by default.

deviceNames.title: Custom accessory names
deviceNames.description: Optional. Override the HomeKit display name per mower, keyed by device id. By default the name is derived automatically (the app nickname if available, otherwise the model with the serial stripped, e.g. 'Yuka'). Example: {"Yuka-MLX9UF6N":"Garden Mower"}.
